name: RustScan (Fast Port Scanner)

auth.notes: No auth; requires network access to target

performance.notes: Scans all 65535 ports in ~3 seconds; passes to nmap for service detection

agent_readiness.notes: Modern fast port scanner that auto-feeds results to nmap. rustscan -a 192.168.1.1 -- -sV -sC (port scan then nmap service detection). --range 1-65535. --batch-size 5000 (concurrent sockets). --timeout 1500. Docker: docker run -it --rm --name rustscan rustscan/rustscan:2.1.1 -a target. Scans all 65535 ports in seconds, then hands open ports to nmap for detailed analysis. Eliminates the slow nmap full port scan step.

gotchas: High batch sizes can trigger IDS/firewall rate limits
Requires nmap installed for service version detection
Docker version recommended to avoid dependency issues
best_when: CTF initial recon (fast full port scan before nmap)
Penetration testing where full port coverage needed quickly
Replace slow nmap -p- full port scans
Combined with nmap for service fingerprinting
avoid_when: When masscan serves better for internet-scale scanning
summary: RustScan scans all 65535 TCP ports in ~3 seconds, then automatically passes open ports to nmap for service detection. Eliminates the slow nmap -p- step. rustscan -a target -- -sV -sC. Docker available. CTF and pentest recon staple. High batch-size setting; tune --batch-size to avoid firewall blocks. Only scan authorized targets.
